threats.title: Weak Password Policy
threats.description: Users may choose weak passwords, making it easier for attackers to gain unauthorized access through brute-force or dictionary attacks. The absence of enforced password complexity and regular password rotation significantly increases the risk.
threats.attack_vector: Brute-force attacks, credential stuffing, dictionary attacks targeting the username/password authentication mechanism.
mitigations: Implement strong password policies (minimum length, complexity requirements, regular password rotation).
Enforce multi-factor authentication (MFA) for all users.
Implement account lockout policies after multiple failed login attempts.

threats.title: Insecure Data Input Handling
threats.description: The system likely processes user input without proper validation and sanitization. This could allow attackers to inject malicious code (e.g., SQL injection, XSS) or manipulate data, leading to data corruption, unauthorized access, or system compromise.
threats.affected_component: All backend services processing user input
threats.attack_vector: Injection attacks (SQLi, XSS, command injection) via vulnerable input fields.
mitigations: Implement robust input validation and sanitization at all stages of data processing.
Use parameterized queries or prepared statements to prevent SQL injection.
Employ a Web Application Firewall (WAF) to filter malicious requests.

threats.title: Default Credentials or Misconfigured Permissions
threats.description: The system may be vulnerable to privilege escalation if default credentials are used, or if user permissions are not properly configured. This could allow an attacker to gain elevated privileges and compromise the entire system.
threats.attack_vector: Exploitation of default credentials, misconfigured user roles, or vulnerabilities in the operating system or application.
mitigations: Change all default credentials immediately.
Implement a robust user role management system with the principle of least privilege.
Regularly review and update user permissions.

threats.title: Unsecured Data Storage
threats.description: Sensitive data (potentially classified based on the unknown internal scheme) may be stored without proper encryption or access controls. This exposes the data to unauthorized access if the system is compromised or if an attacker gains access to the storage.
threats.attack_vector: Unauthorized access to database or file storage due to weak access controls, misconfigured permissions, or vulnerabilities in the storage system.
mitigations: Encrypt all sensitive data at rest and in transit.
Implement strict access controls based on the principle of least privilege.
Regularly audit access permissions and data storage configurations.

threats.title: Lack of Audit Logging
threats.description: Insufficient logging and monitoring make it difficult to track user activity, identify security incidents, and hold individuals accountable for their actions. This severely hinders forensic investigations and the ability to prove or disprove malicious activity.
threats.attack_vector: Attackers can operate undetected, making it impossible to trace their actions or identify the source of a breach.
mitigations: Implement comprehensive logging of all critical system events (user logins, data access, configuration changes).
Establish a centralized logging system with retention policies.
Regularly review logs for suspicious activity and establish alerting mechanisms.

mapped_threats.title: Weak Password Policy
mapped_threats.description: Users may choose weak passwords, making it easier for attackers to gain unauthorized access through brute-force or dictionary attacks. The absence of enforced password complexity and regular password rotation significantly increases the risk.
mapped_threats.attack_vector: Brute-force attacks, credential stuffing, dictionary attacks targeting the username/password authentication mechanism.

Enforce multi-factor authentication (MFA) for all users.

mapped_threats.title: Lack of Audit Logging
mapped_threats.description: Insufficient logging and monitoring make it difficult to track user activity, identify security incidents, and hold individuals accountable for their actions. This severely hinders forensic investigations and the ability to prove or disprove malicious activity.

gaps: Weak Password Policy - Insufficient logging and monitoring of password changes.
Unsecured Data Storage - Lack of encryption and access controls beyond basic storage protection.
priorities: High - Elevation of Privilege and Insecure Data Input Handling due to critical severity and potential for widespread impact.
Medium - Weak Password Policy and Lack of Audit Logging -  Requires immediate attention but impact is less immediate than the critical threats.

metadata.error: AnalysisRepository.add_log() got an unexpected keyword argument 'agent_name'

dfd_mermaid:
flowchart TB

    %% Styling
    classDef external fill:#e1f5fe,stroke:#0277bd,stroke-width:2px
    classDef process fill:#e8f5e9,stroke:#2e7d32,stroke-width:2px
    classDef datastore fill:#fff3e0,stroke:#ef6c00,stroke-width:2px
    classDef threat fill:#ffebee,stroke:#c62828,stroke-width:3px

    subgraph External["External Entities"]
        user[("👤 User")]
        admin[("👔 Admin")]
        external_api[("🌐 External API")]
    end

    subgraph TrustBoundary["Trust Boundary"]
        subgraph Frontend["Frontend Layer"]
            web_app["🖥️ Web Application"]
        end

        subgraph Backend["Backend Layer"]
            api_gateway["🚪 API Gateway"]
            auth_service["🔐 Auth Service"]
            core_api["⚙️ Core API"]
        end

        subgraph Data["Data Layer"]
            database[("💾 Database")]
            cache[("⚡ Cache")]
        end
    end

    %% Data Flows
    user -->|"🔒 HTTPS"| web_app
    admin -->|"🔒 HTTPS + MFA"| web_app
    web_app -->|"🔒 API Calls"| api_gateway
    api_gateway -->|"🔒 Auth Check"| auth_service
    api_gateway -->|"🔒 Business Logic"| core_api
    auth_service -->|"🔒 User Data"| database
    core_api -->|"🔒 CRUD"| database
    core_api -->|"⚠️ Session"| cache
    core_api -->|"🔒 External Call"| external_api

    %% Apply Styles
    class user,admin,external_api external
    class web_app,api_gateway,auth_service,core_api process
    class database,cache datastore
    class auth_service,core_api,database threat

semgrep.config_file:
rules:
- id: security-custom-1
  languages:
  - python
  - javascript
  - typescript
  message: Weak Password Policy
  metadata:
    category: security
    confidence: HIGH
    cwe: CWE-287
    owasp: A07:2021
    threat_category: Spoofing
  patterns:
  - pattern: password = "..."
  - pattern: secret = "..."
  - pattern: api_key = "..."
  severity: ERROR
- fix: cursor.execute($QUERY, (params,))
  id: security-custom-2
  languages:
  - python
  - javascript
  - typescript
  message: Insecure Data Input Handling
  metadata:
    category: security
    confidence: HIGH
    cwe: CWE-89
    owasp: A03:2021
    threat_category: Tampering
  patterns:
  - pattern: cursor.execute($QUERY)
    pattern-not: cursor.execute($QUERY, $PARAMS)
  severity: ERROR
- id: security-custom-3
  languages:
  - python
  - javascript
  - typescript
  message: Default Credentials or Misconfigured Permissions
  metadata:
    category: security
    confidence: HIGH
    cwe: CWE-269
    owasp: A01:2021
    threat_category: Elevation of Privilege
  patterns:
  - pattern: eval($X)
  - pattern: exec($X)
  severity: ERROR
- id: security-custom-4
  languages:
  - python
  - javascript
  - typescript
  message: Unsecured Data Storage
  metadata:
    category: security
    confidence: HIGH
    cwe: CWE-200
    owasp: A01:2021
    threat_category: Information Disclosure
  patterns:
  - pattern: print($SENSITIVE)
  - pattern: console.log($SENSITIVE)
  severity: ERROR
- id: security-custom-5
  languages:
  - python
  - javascript
  - typescript
  message: Lack of Audit Logging
  metadata:
    category: security
    confidence: HIGH
    cwe: CWE-778
    owasp: A09:2021
    threat_category: Repudiation
  patterns:
  - pattern: eval($X)
  - pattern: exec($X)
  severity: ERROR
